subject: Biology
chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

questions.q: What is the main objective of crop improvement in agriculture?
questions.answer: The main objective of crop improvement is to develop crop varieties that are high-yielding, disease-resistant, and adaptable to different environmental conditions.

questions.q: Which of the following is a technique used for introducing desirable traits into crops? (a) Embryo rescue (b) Somatic hybridization (c) Marker-assisted selection (d) All of the above
Explain the role of biofertilizers in enhancing food production.
questions.answer: Biofertilizers are living organisms that help to increase crop production by improving soil fertility. They include Rhizobium, Azospirillum, and Azotobacter, which fix nitrogen in the soil, reducing the need for chemical fertilizers. Biofertilizers also produce plant growth-promoting substances, which enhance plant growth and development.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Plant breeding involves the use of _______________ to produce new crop varieties.
What is the difference between traditional breeding and modern breeding techniques?
questions.answer: Traditional breeding involves the use of conventional methods such as selection, hybridization, and mutation breeding, whereas modern breeding techniques involve the use of biotechnology tools such as genetic engineering and marker-assisted selection.

questions.q: True or False: Hydroponics is a technique of growing plants in soil.
Explain the concept of tissue culture and its application in plant breeding.
questions.answer: Tissue culture is a technique of growing plants in a controlled environment using tissue or cells. It involves the use of a nutrient medium to support plant growth. Tissue culture has several applications in plant breeding, including the production of disease-free plants, rapid multiplication of plants, and the production of hybrid seeds.

questions.q: Which of the following is an advantage of intercropping? (a) Increased pest incidence (b) Improved soil fertility (c) Reduced crop yield (d) Increased water requirement
What is the role of embryo rescue in plant breeding?
questions.answer: Embryo rescue is a technique used to rescue embryos from wide crosses, which would otherwise abort due to incompatibility between the parents. It involves the culture of embryos in a nutrient medium to produce viable plants.

questions.q: Explain the importance of crop rotation in enhancing food production.
questions.answer: Crop rotation is the practice of growing different crops on the same land in a sequential manner. It helps to improve soil fertility, reduce soil erosion, and increase crop yields. Crop rotation also helps to break the life cycle of pests and diseases, reducing the need for pesticides and other chemicals.
